ANT 104 - INTRO TO BIO ANTHRO
Home Genetic Diet Social Bonds Conclusion Citations
Conclusion

Conclusion

Summary of Findings

My research aimed to find how human hunting to farming impacted the biological foundations of dogs. While humans were breeding for appearances and luxury since the Victorian era, this research shows human cultural evolution altered dogs' habits and diets for thousands of years.

Answering My Core Questions

  • When and Why: Dogs were domesticated during the Paleolithic era societies agriculture era defining change. They originated from an "ancestry distinct from present-day wolves" (Bergström 1).
  • Social Relationship: The bond between human and their best companions was emotional and not based on pure function, the Bonn-Oberkassel burial proves this. Human farming did change the relationships however as dogs were no longer hunting companions.
  • Biological Impacts on Dogs: The switch of lifestyles forced a genomic mutation and increase of the AMY2B gene, allowing dogs the ability to digest the starch-rich crops human farms grew.
